dataList.name: Story Dungeon Starting Choice Event
dataList.desc:
We emerged into a dark and muggy cavern, filled with air so stale it almost felt stifling.

Up ahead, a thin ray of light shone through the darkness, seemingly guiding our path. Following the light led us to a four-way fork in the road.

A constant, powerful gust howled through one of the paths. Another was filled with a metallic tang—the smell of blood. Then, there was a path that was entirely silent, and the last one echoed with the harsh noise of sword striking against sword.

Which path should we choose?

options.messageDesc: Select to gain a Poise E.G.O Gift
result:
"W-wow, the wind's... pretty... strong in here...!"

The wind only got more turbulent the deeper we progressed; by the time we were nearing the end of the passage, the Sinners could barely keep their eyes open and had to cover them with their arms.

Then, all of a sudden... tranquility arrived, the wind settling as though it hadn't been blasting us in the face just a moment ago.

The dust the wind kicked up dissipated, and revealed a scarlet rope haphazardly thrown atop a pedestal made of stacked stones.

options.message: The path that smells of blood.
options.messageDesc: Select to gain a Bleed E.G.O Gift
result:
"The scent of blood... saturates the very air..."
"Oi, lass! Pull yourself together, yeah? Don't wait too long to take another one of them blood packs if you need it."
"... 'Tis hardly enough to shake my resolve."

But as we progressed through the passage, the scent of blood only got more oppressive, and we were starting to stumble from the sheer thickness of it... Until all of a sudden, the air cleared to a fresh crispness.

As we were getting our bearings, I noticed a pedestal of stone stacked before us.

On top of it lay a neat briefcase that seemed to have been well-cared for.

options.messageDesc: Select to gain a Keywordless E.G.O Gift
result:
The passage was quiet, but that only intensified the uncanny dread pervading its silence.

Nervous, the Sinners progressed through the passage accompanied by the absolute lack of sound save for the deep echoes of their own footsteps... until we reached what appeared to be a pedestal made of stacked stones.

On top of the pedestal was something we'd become familiar with at this point—a can made for inhaling.

options.message: The path loud with the sound of clashing swords.
options.messageDesc: Select to gain a Slash E.G.O Gift
result:
"Just how many people are fighting up there...?"
"We must proceed with caution, Executive Manager. There may be a battle underway."

The Sinners all looked disconcerted at the strident noise of metal against metal; it was an unmistakable sound of battle.

We stood ready in troubled silence as the sounds of war only grew louder and louder... until they came to a sudden, silent stop.

A pedestal made of stacked stones appeared before us, though none of us knew how long it had been there.

Atop it was a red accessory, the kind one would hang at the bottom of the hilt.

dataList.name: Bamboo-hatted Kim's Past 1
dataList.desc:
"Um, excuse me..."

As we were slowly looking around the streets of S Corp., a youth, after fidgeting and hesitating for a moment, walked up to us and asked for help.

The youth explained that, wishing to better his own status, he took the Royal Examination—akin to a public employment policy in S Corp.—and was able to attain a high grade for himself.

And that was where his problems began; as far as he knew, people who passed the exam with flying colors like himself had their travel expenses paid for the homeward trip and were to be granted a guide. But there must've been a misunderstanding or something, because when he left the exam grounds, no one was there to escort him.

Confused by the unexpected turn of events, he wandered away from the exam grounds in search of his escort, lost his bearings, and found himself in these alleys.

He's asking if we could help him with directions... What should we do?

options.message: "Sorry, we've never been here before, either."
result:
"Ah, you too are unfamiliar with these streets?"

The youth wandered off, clicking his tongue in disappointment.

"... Hmmh."

As soon as the youth disappeared from our sight, Bamboo-hatted Kim, who had slinked away to the back of the group, suddenly let out a deep sigh.

"Have you aught to say, Mentor?"
"Uh, that's... me. I am that youth."

... The sudden reveal threw the Sinners for a brief loop.

"Back then, I was a conceited fool who proudly smelled his own farts, not knowing how ridiculous he appeared."
"The exam was to compose an essay on a certain someone who has committed treason against S Corp. Full of foolish confidence as always, I wrote them a long paper humiliating the treasonous wretch so that no such embarrassment could ever stain our history again..."
"... and only upon arriving home did I realize who he was. Who would've known that the 'treasonous wretch' I had excoriated savagely in the exam was none other than my own ancestor?"

"T-then the reason you did not seek out a seat of an official, even after passing the test, was..."

"How could I dare, Aeng-du? Why do you think no escort volunteered to guide my foolish past self?"
"Because every soul in S Corp. but myself already knew what I had done, who I was—a bastard who betrayed his own ancestor's name to pass an exam."

Kim muttered placidly as though he was sharing a mildly funny joke, then walked ahead of us...

"But they needn't have shamed me into breaking my brush, for I had already done that myself."

... while quickly hiding his shaking hand beneath the other.

options.message: "Let me ask my group if they can help."
options.messageDesc: Run a Gluttony & Pride Advantage check to gain a Keywordless E.G.O Gift.

dataList.name: Bamboo-hatted Kim's Past 2
dataList.desc: Navigating our way through the streets of S Corp., we come across a young man hugging a blade, his gaze wandering blankly toward the sky. "... Ah." He seemed to have caught us staring—he turned his head toward us, gave us a once-over, then slumped back where he sat. "Pass on by, folks. I have no intent of bringing you harm." His voice was familiar... but I couldn't quite place my finger on it. "Search not for a revelation in the clouds drifting by, for they hold no answers." Kim blurted out of the blue as we were about to walk past the sword-hugging man. Oh, then that man must be... "... Do you know me?" "Must I know you to impart some unsolicited advice? A meddler in me simply cannot help himself when walking past a disadvantageous game of baduk." The man widened his eyes in brief surprise, then began in a contemplative voice. "You are a player of greater dan than I—that I can tell; so if you will, permit me to solicit some advice." "Shamed I am of my past plays, for they were dishonorable and deserving of ridicule; thus I had begun to wonder if I would be of better use should I become a blade for hire..." "Then, a man of incorruptible integrity, a just soul, sought me out and asked me that I serve as his blade. Yet, I worry that my filth may befoul his hands, too." "What say you? Would you say that I take this opportunity and make this play? Or..."

options.messageDesc: Select to gain a Slash E.G.O Gift
result:
"Shamed you may be of your past plays, yet what of it?"

"What choice have you got but to continue placing the stones, when the game shall avail you no mercy of reverting a move?"

"What if continuing this play leads me to lose all of my territories?"

"Then resign the game."
"You've given it all to the end, and if it is indeed a bitter one, then what can you do? The least you can do is play it until the game is called."
"You're playing by following the flow anyhow, aren't you?"

"......."
"Though we have but just met, it seems as though your gaze penetrates deep into my bones, stranger."
"Take this as a token of my thanks for your 'meddling.'"

A lightbulb seemed to have gone off in his head; light flashed in the eyes of the man, or Bamboo-hatted Kim of the past, and he jumped to his feet and sped off, but not before handing us something.

"... Not that you'll be able to do anything to change the course of this losing game."
I think I heard Kim whisper.

options.messageDesc: Run a Sloth & Pride Advantage check to heal the selected Identity.

dataList.name: Bamboo-hatted Kim's Past 3
dataList.desc:
I catch a glimpse of a figure, staring off into the darkness of the unlit prison.

"Hah..."

Bamboo-hatted Kim lets out a sigh, but before he even fully exhales...

"Who goes there?!"

A sharp cry cuts through the stagnant air. Someone here must've noticed us.

What should we do?

options.message: Claim that we are the prison's guards, keeping our eyes on the prisoner.
result:
"At least put a little effort into your lies, huh?!"

Clearly, pretending to be prison guards the way we are was a lie too easily disproven...

We took to our heels as the woman, red with anger, came after us with her sword unsheathed.

options.message: Unsheathe our weapons and stand ready for battle.
options.messageDesc: Gain a Slash E.G.O Gift depending on the result of the following choice
result:
"... You're not the prison guards."

The woman who called us stood there for a moment, nonchalantly trying to gauge us even as we stood with our weapons drawn. Then, she walked toward us in a gait that could not be called aggressive.

But instead of chatting with us, she called upon the figure sitting inside the prison.

"Would you care to explain? Who are they? And... what happened?"

The one-sided exchange continued for a while as she tried various lines of questioning to chat up the figure inside the cell, but it was to no avail.

"... Hah. Might as well be talking to a corpse long dead."

She let out an exasperated sigh before turning to us.

dataList.name: Bamboo-hatted Kim's Past 3
dataList.desc: "Are you not here to visit him as well? Might you be those who share his purpose?"

result:
"... You're not?"
"Then might I... consider you naught but spies who have infiltrated the prison of the Royal Court?"

We took to our heels as the woman, without giving us even a second to explain ourselves, charged at us with her weapon drawn.

options.message: Say that you are, and explain the situation.
options.messageDesc: Run a Lust & Pride Advantage check to gain a Slash E.G.O Gift.

dataList.name: Bamboo-hatted Kim's Past 4
dataList.desc:
Someone carrying a sword on his belt and a sack on his shoulder leaped into a haystack near us.

A few moments later, a small squad of Ch'unokkuns, torches in hand, rushed in from the same direction the man had come from.

"We saw a criminal who has coveted the Kingdom's properties flee in this direction. Speak up if you have seen the wretch!"

The Ch'unokkuns began walking up to the civilians and questioning them about the criminal's whereabouts. Despite all having seen the man jump into the haystack, all of them shake their heads and refuse to give up the criminal.

Are they hiding him for some reason?

Soon, the Ch'unokkuns, having made their way through the crowd, approached us as well.

What should we do?

options.message: Say that you don't know, then slowly turn toward the haystack.
result:
"Hmmmmm..."

As the Ch'unokkuns began to surround the haystack, the man who had been hiding in there suddenly burst out and bolted away in the opposite direction.

"T-that's him!"

The Ch'unokkuns briefly froze in stunned silence, then began rushing toward where the man had fled.

... Something about the way the civilians were glaring at us made it clear they didn't like that.

options.message: Point them to the wrong direction.
options.messageDesc: Run a Pride & Envy Advantage check to heal HP and SP for all Identities.

dataList.name: Blade Lineage
dataList.desc:
A man with a sword hanging from his belt was wrestling an old woman, trying to take the bags she was carrying.

It soon became clear from their arguments that the old woman was the man's mother.

Though it seemed that the old woman was trying to reprimand her son, he responded by manhandling her and refused to let go of her bags.

"Should we do naught and watch, that man will surely injure the old woman...!"

Should we get involved?

options.message: Threaten the sword-carrying man and get him to leave.
options.messageDesc: Run a Wrath & Pride Advantage check to heal HP for all Identities.

options.message: Attack the sword-carrying man and get him off his mother.
result: Preoccupied with wrestling his mother, the man did not see us coming—he allowed a strike to his back. "Agh, sh-shit...!" The man immediately turned tail and disappeared into the dark alleys. We approached the old woman with her things, hoping that we could at least give her peace of mind... "So you cut my son with a weapon?! What is wrong with you people?!" ... but had to take a step back as she began to furiously smack us on our backs. We tried to explain to her that the best way to stop him from trying to manhandle her was to scare him straight by giving him a light flesh wound, but it seemed that the old woman was done trying to hear us out. We returned to our original journey, trying our best not to let the old woman's curses get to us...
